answer:
The Snowflake-managed MCP server is a fully managed service that allows AI agents to securely interact with Snowflake data and Cortex AI services without needing separate infrastructure [1][2]. It acts as a governed gateway, exposing tools like Cortex Analyst, Cortex Search, Cortex Agents, and SQL execution while enforcing Snowflake's existing OAuth authentication and role-based access control [1][3].

To create an MCP server, use the CREATE MCP SERVER command with a YAML specification defining the desired tools [4].

```sql
CREATE MCP SERVER my_mcp_server
  FROM SPECIFICATION $$
    tools:
      - name: "my-search"
        type: "CORTEX_SEARCH_SERVICE_QUERY"
        identifier: "database.schema.service_name"
        description: "Search service description"
        title: "Search"
  $$;
```

This managed service supports the MCP revision 2025-11-25 [1]. Users should avoid deprecated community-run projects in favor of this official, Snowflake-supported solution [5].
